creative_brief: Earthy, editorial feel. Hero image of dark soil with carrots being pulled — raw, unpolished. Single green CTA button. Minimal layout, generous whitespace, no stock photography.
subject: The morning I almost didn't send this
preheader: Our VIP list closes soon. $5 refundable deposit locks in your spot.
body:
Hi {first_name},

At 6:14am last Tuesday, I stood in a paddock outside Mudgee watching a farmer named Glen pull carrots from soil so dark it looked like coffee grounds.

He turned to me and said, "Rachel, I've got enough crop for maybe 200 families this season. That's it."

That's the thing nobody talks about. Regenerative farms don't scale like factories. Glen's carrots taste like actual carrots because his soil has been rebuilt over seven years. But seven years of care means a finite harvest.

This is why our VIP window matters.

When we open our equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal mid-2026, VIP members get first access. First to invest. First to own a piece of what we're building — a farm-direct grocery store where families like yours buy from farmers like Glen.

Here's what's real: we work with around 15-20 farms and suppliers across NSW. Each one has limits. Our hub-and-collect model from Brookvale keeps costs low and cuts the 2,400km supermarket supply chain down to almost nothing. But capacity has a ceiling.

The VIP list has a ceiling too.

For a $5 refundable deposit, you lock in:

— First access to invest (minimum $50, up to $10,000)
— Priority updates before anyone else
— Exclusive Q&A sessions with our team
— A fully refundable deposit if you change your mind

No commitment beyond that. No subscription. No lock-in. When we launch, you order what you want, when you want, and collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

But the VIP window won't stay open indefinitely. Once we hit our number, it closes.

Glen's carrots don't wait. Neither does this.

Join the VIP list →

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
